_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Local batch-44: 4-pack evidence-finalize (rick-west-chesapeake VA Mayor + 3 FL councilmembers Hearn / Gabbard / Gerdes). Headline catches: (a) rick-west-chesapeake slug-collision unblocked in standalone commit; preserved research applied here. (b) andre-dickens FL ghost deduped in standalone commit (GA canonical already evidence_local). (c) Brandi Gabbard term-limited + running for St Pete Mayor 2026 vs Welch — currency upgrade. (d) Copley Gerdes is St Pete Council CHAIR (since Dec 2024) and the YES architect on Rays stadium bonds + 2026 Pride flag-raising. (e) Lavander Hearn sparse single-cell record (FY22 + FY25 millage hikes only researchable position). All TRUE/FALSE cells cite a primary URL; party-default presumptions held null; St Pete races are officially NONPARTISAN ballots (personal party reg noted in background).

profile.background: Dr. Richard Wayne 'Rick' West. Mayor of City of Chesapeake, VA since November 14, 2017 (initially appointed when prior mayor stepped down; won 2018 special election 57.81%, re-elected 2020 with 65.99%, re-elected November 5, 2024). Current term ends January 1, 2029. Public-school administrator in Chesapeake Public Schools for 20+ years (holds doctorate); served 10 years on Chesapeake City Council before becoming mayor. Born and raised in rural Chesapeake; father served U.S. Navy.

rick-west-chesapeake.notes_set: Mayor of Chesapeake, VA (R) since Nov 2017; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 (term to Jan 1, 2029). [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement — slug-collision RESOLVED batch-44 via disambiguate-rick-west.py (rick-west -> rick-west-chesapeake); OK State Rep keeps canonical slug]. HARD evidence: (a) presided over Dec 10 2019 unanimous Chesapeake City Council adoption of a 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution and personally pledged 'we will not just tell you to go to Richmond, we will go to Richmond with you' to constituents opposing Northam-era state gun bills (self_defense q0/q1 TRUE); (b) issued Chesapeake's FIRST-EVER Pride Month proclamation in June 2024 from the dais ('I Richard West, Mayor of the City of Chesapeake, do hereby proclaim the month of June as PRIDE MONTH') (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — R-DEVIATION); (c) FY25 budget held real-estate tax rate flat at $1.01/$100 and FY26 proposed rate increase was 'no longer being considered' after public pushback (economic_stewardship q2 TRUE on nominal rate; rising assessments yielded effective-rate growth but the rate itself was held). NO EVIDENCE on abortion, family/school, Christian liberty, election administration, COVID-mandate resistance, ESG, or immigration at the mayoral level — those cells null. Affirmative public 'Back the Blue' advocacy not located — public_justice q0 null (routine budget funding alone does not clear the rubric's high bar). Religion / church affiliation not publicly stated. Flag: August 2024 WHRO report that West asked city attorney to 'look into' a personal legal matter for his stepbrother (Georgia property dispute) drew an ethics-flag and a councilman's resignation call — noted as governance-conduct context, not a rubric-scoring event.

4.note: Mayor West personally issued Chesapeake's first-ever Pride Month proclamation, June 2024, from the dais at a city council meeting.

2.note: FY25 budget held real-estate tax rate flat at $1.01/$100; FY26 proposed rate increase was withdrawn after public pushback.

0.note: Presided over Dec 10 2019 unanimous Chesapeake City Council adoption of a Second Amendment 'Constitutional City' resolution; pledged 'we will not just tell you to go to Richmond, we will go to Richmond with you.'

1.note: Same 2019 2A-sanctuary resolution opposing Northam-era state red-flag / AWB / magazine-limit bills; mayor publicly led the local-control pushback.

profile.background: Lavander Hearn. City of Cocoa, Florida City Council District 2 since November 2020; selected Deputy Mayor unanimously by colleagues November 9, 2021 (rotating designation among councilmembers). Race: Black. Cocoa municipal elections are officially NONPARTISAN ballots; personal voter registration Democrat (Brevard County Democratic Party 'Elected Officials' list). Filed for re-election January 23, 2024; no challenger surfaced; current term continues. Education: AA Fire Science, Eastern Florida State College; BBA; MA Emergency Management / Disaster Mitigation. Career: Brevard County Fire Rescue Firefighter/EMT; substitute teacher with Brevard Public Schools. Cocoa native (Cocoa High alumnus); active in Cocoa Masonic Lodge #55. Father; engaged in youth mentorship; profiles note he 'faced legal challenges as a teenager' but was redirected through mentorship. No public church or denominational affiliation.

lavander-hearn.notes_set: City of Cocoa, FL D2 councilmember (NP ballot, personal D registration); sworn Nov 2020, Deputy Mayor since Nov 2021; re-election filed Jan 23, 2024 (no challenger surfaced). [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] SPARSE-DATA candidate — only one rubric-relevant cell findable. HARD evidence: economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — sat on the council that voted 4-1 in Sept 2021 to raise Cocoa millage from 5.579 to 6.453 (he is not identified as the dissenter); supported the FY2025 tentative millage of 6.9532 (9.05% over rollback rate of 6.3761) — engaged process by suggesting 'scenario-based presentations' but did not vote against the increase. Pattern: two documented millage hikes during his tenure, no recorded NO vote — clear non-stewardship pattern. NULL on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (no Cocoa Pride proclamation surfaced; rubric requires affirmative action either direction), family_child_sovereignty (Brevard School Board is separate authority), christian_liberty (no church affiliation publicly stated), election_integrity, public_justice (no individual pro-police statement; budget funding alone does not clear the high bar), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration, and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts city gun rules). No ethical or legal controversies surfaced in 2024-2026 reporting.

2.note: Sept 2021 Cocoa Council 4-1 raised millage 5.579 -> 6.453 (Hearn not identified as the dissenter); FY2025 tentative millage 6.9532 (9.05% over rollback 6.3761) — Hearn engaged process by suggesting scenario-based presentations but did not vote against the increase. Two documented millage hikes during his tenure, no recorded NO vote.

profile.background: Brandi Gabbard. City of St. Petersburg, Florida City Council District 2 (NE St. Pete — Barcley Estates / Northeast) since January 2018; re-elected unopposed 2021; current term ends January 7, 2027 (TERM-LIMITED). Officially NONPARTISAN ballot; personal voter registration Democrat. Filed March 2026 to run for St. Petersburg MAYOR in November 2026, challenging incumbent Mayor Ken Welch — positioning to the LEFT of Welch on DEI/LGBTQ issues. Past Council Chair. Indiana native; moved to St. Petersburg 2003 with husband Keith; one son in Barcley Estates. Real-estate broker-associate at Smith & Associates (also Suncoast Realty Solutions); 18+ years in Tampa Bay, $70M+ in sales. 2014 President, Pinellas Realtor Organization; 2015 Pinellas Realtor of the Year; 2018 Chair, NAR Insurance Committee (testified before FL Governor and Cabinet on flood insurance). Religion / church affiliation not publicly stated.

brandi-gabbard.notes_set: St Petersburg City Council D2 (NP ballot, personal D registration); term to Jan 7, 2027 — TERM-LIMITED; filed March 2026 for 2026 St Pete Mayor race vs incumbent Welch (candidacy_status running_higher_office). [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] HARD evidence: (a) sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE — Feb 23, 2023 Council Chair Gabbard initially SUPPORTED a $50,000 city allocation to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund; on April 6, 2023 reversed and voted against it but explicitly cited political-risk calculus (state-Republican retaliation threats, Disney/Andrew Warren examples), NOT pro-life conviction. Initial pro-fund support is the dispositive position. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — attended/participated in June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising at City Hall during 'Shine with Pride 2026'; posted official 'Happy Pride St Pete' on D2 Facebook; as mayoral candidate (April 2026) condemned FL anti-DEI law SB 1134 ('every resident — regardless of their race or whom they love — has an equal seat at the table'). (c) economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — voted YES on the $6.5B Rays Stadium / Gas Plant District deal July 18, 2024 (5-3) AND on the Dec 5, 2024 bond authorization (long-term city debt for a private-team stadium). Quote: 'We need to move this forward because, quite frankly, I think we're calling their bluff by doing so.' Also supports general-obligation infrastructure bonds (future-generation debt justified by resilience needs). NULL on family/school (Pinellas school board is separate authority), christian_liberty (no public stance; religion not stated), election_integrity (Pinellas SoE handles elections), public_justice (no affirmative pro-police statement located; mayoral 'public safety' platform pillar lacks specifics), refuse_state_overreach (her anti-DEI/anti-preemption stance defends progressive priorities — not the rubric's conservative-subsidiarity anchor; held null), border_immigration (no Gabbard-specific ICE/sanctuary statement located; she joined council Jan 2018 after the 2017 sanctuary-city statement under Mayor Kriseman), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts).

1.note: Feb 23, 2023 Council Chair Gabbard initially SUPPORTED $50,000 city allocation to Tampa Bay Abortion Fund; April 6, 2023 reversal was explicitly political-risk calculus (state retaliation threats), not pro-life conviction. Initial pro-fund support is the dispositive position.

4.note: Attended/participated in June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising at St Pete City Hall; official D2 Facebook 'Happy Pride St Pete' post; condemned FL anti-DEI law SB 1134 as mayoral candidate.

2.note: Voted YES on $6.5B Rays Stadium / Gas Plant District deal July 18, 2024 (5-3) AND on Dec 5, 2024 bond authorization (long-term city debt for private-team stadium). Also supports general-obligation infrastructure bonds.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (St. Petersburg Catholic High School alumnus; B.A. in Religion with Catholic Studies emphasis from Saint Leo University, a Catholic institution; personal faith is documented but no specific policy position derived from it)
profile.background: Charles 'Copley' Gerdes. City of St. Petersburg, Florida City Council District 1 since January 6, 2022 (special election to replace Robert Blackmon); re-elected UNOPPOSED Nov 5, 2024; current term ends January 4, 2029. Officially NONPARTISAN ballot. Council CHAIR since December 2024 (elected after five rounds of voting); also Chair of Budget, Finance & Taxation Committee. Listed #21 on Florida Politics' Tampa Bay Most Powerful Politicians. Family: son of former St. Pete Councilmember Charlie Gerdes (also held D1) and nephew of city administrator Rob Gerdes. Education: St. Petersburg Catholic HS; B.A. Religion (Catholic Studies emphasis), Saint Leo University. Profession: financial advisor with Northwestern Mutual since 2011; also Director of Special Projects / Strategic Development at family law firm Keane, Reese, Vesely & Gerdes P.A. Married Cecily Valenty (2016); two children. Catholic faith publicly documented via education path but no derived policy position scored.

copley-gerdes.notes_set: St Petersburg City Council D1 since Jan 6, 2022; re-elected UNOPPOSED Nov 5, 2024; current term to Jan 4, 2029; Council CHAIR since Dec 2024 (also Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair). Officially NP ballot. [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] HARD evidence: (a) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — as Council Chair, PERSONALLY INTRODUCED the June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising ceremony at City Hall (Tampa Bay Times / Watermark Out News); in October 2025 voted YES to advance Councilmember Driscoll's 'Harmony' (LGBTQ+) and 'History' (Black history) city-flag variations (joining Driscoll, Givens, Figgs-Sanders, Floyd, Hanewicz; only Harting voted no). His own quote: 'I want to throw a haymaker… I don't want to throw a jab.' (b) economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — as Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair voted YES on the FY24 city budget that Catalyst headlined as 'begrudgingly approves budget, 13% tax increase'; YES on the $287.5M Rays stadium bonds + $142M Gas Plant District bonds (4-3, July 2024); YES on the original 5-3 stadium agreement. Pattern: chairs the budget committee, votes for major debt issuance + tax increases. NULL on sanctity_of_life (no St Pete pro-life resolution; no Gerdes statement on abortion), family_child_sovereignty (Pinellas school board is separate authority), christian_liberty (Catholic faith documented in profile.religion but no policy vote — religion is biographical not scoreable on its own per evidence-first rule), election_integrity (no Gerdes-specific election-admin vote), public_justice (CALL — Community Assistance & Life Liaison — is a partial-diversion social-worker-first model that diverts certain 911 calls FROM sworn officers; Gerdes praised CALL participation; no affirmative pro-police statement clears the rubric's high bar), refuse_state_overreach (his pro-LGBTQ-flag stance opposing DeSantis state actions defends progressive priorities — not the rubric's conservative-subsidiarity anchor; held null), border_immigration (the 2017 'sanctuary city' comments were from his FATHER Charlie Gerdes, NOT Copley — do not attribute), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts). DISCIPLINE FLAGS: father/son distinction matters (Charlie Gerdes was the 2017 sanctuary-city commenter); Catholic-school + Catholic-university religion degree is biographically suggestive but NOT scored as a policy verdict per evidence-first rule.

4.note: As Council Chair, personally INTRODUCED the June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising ceremony at City Hall; in October 2025 voted YES to advance Driscoll's LGBTQ+ 'Harmony' and Black-history 'History' city-flag variations. Own quote: 'I want to throw a haymaker… I don't want to throw a jab.'

2.note: As Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair voted YES on FY24 budget that approved a 13% tax increase ('begrudgingly approves' per Catalyst); YES on $287.5M Rays stadium bonds + $142M Gas Plant District bonds (4-3, July 2024); YES on the original 5-3 stadium agreement.
